summary: W3-K batch 1 (single PR): polish the Fabric MCP server tool shape across 3 locked behaviors — K4 single-line content[].text summaries (eliminate the 19KB double-payload), K5 action_hint on scope-regex validation errors (reuse payload-warning vocabulary), K6 upgrade fab_recall's bare omitted_candidate_count to a structured dropped[]{id,reason} form reusing fab_archive_scan's dropped[]{session_id,reason-enum} template. All work lives in packages/server/src/tools/ + packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts. TDD: each K-item is a RED→GREEN→REFACTOR chain. Tasks are serialized into a single wave because K4 and K6 both edit recall.ts and all three touch api-contracts.ts — concurrent edits would collide.
approach: One task per locked behavior (K4=TASK-001, K5=TASK-002, K6=TASK-003), no over-splitting (K4 touches 4 tool files but is ONE task). Execute strictly sequentially (single wave, sequential within wave) to avoid concurrent edits to the shared api-contracts.ts and recall.ts. Each task is test-first: write/extend the failing test in the named *.test.ts (RED), implement the minimal change (GREEN), then refactor onto the shared helper (payload-warning.ts for K5, archive-scan's dropped[] shape for K6). Schema changes in api-contracts.ts REQUIRE `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared build` before server vitest, else runtime invalid_union_discriminator (known recurring pitfall). Final gate per task: tsc --noEmit 0 + fabric-server vitest green.

stages: K4: each tool handler builds structuredContent (unchanged) + content[].text (now single-line summary)
K5: scope-regex (SCOPE_COORDINATE_PATTERN) validation failure -> structured error envelope with action_hint containing a legal example (project:fabric-v2)
K6: recall service emits dropped[]{id,reason} (controlled reason vocabulary) -> recallOutputSchema -> migrate consumers/docs of omitted_candidate_count

design_decisions: Serialize all 3 tasks (single sequential wave): K4+K6 both edit recall.ts, all 3 edit api-contracts.ts — parallel waves would cause merge collisions on shared files.
K4 is ONE task across 4 tool files (recall/archive-scan/extract-knowledge/review) — anti-splitting rule: one behavior = one task even at 4-file fan-out.
K5 reuses the existing payload-warning.ts action_hint vocabulary (StructuredToolWarning.action_hint) — do NOT invent a parallel error-hint system.

K6 reuses fab_archive_scan's dropped[]{session_id, reason:z.enum([...])} shape (api-contracts.ts:572-577) verbatim as the template; recall uses {id, reason} with ONE shared reason vocabulary across tools.
Schema edits gated behind `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-shared build` (rebuild dist) before any server test run — recurring invalid_union_discriminator pitfall.

patterns: dropped[]{key, reason: z.enum(...)} controlled-vocabulary drop record
conventions: zod schemas in packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts; dist must be rebuilt after schema edits
